code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test18-JAN-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function with possible tubular involvement. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia may indicate recent hypoglycemia or altered red cell turnover. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
